Let me tell you a story. I tried Monster iSport Immersion with control talk, Klipsch Image S4i, Sony XBA-4IP and Boss MIE2i.

Each of these midrange earphones, and Sony XBA-4IP as of high range are totally different in their sound quality, comfort, quality and feel in various ways.
I tried Monster iSport, which seals sound in a better way than rest of others, but sounds mix of everything emphasizing bass. Then I tried Klipsch Image S4i, which is comparatively a huge improvement on comfort and feel along with considerably admirable bass and effective vocal clarity.

But, it didn't satisfy me because I was in search of a better earphones that I can have all what I would expect from. Then I went to buy Boss and then Sony. All piece of shit. Nothing that is worth to glorify: hey, I've these earphones! You will lose every penny you spent on them and never be able to compensate with these all brands I mentioned unless one maker comes ahead and delivers what we really want.

In my totally personal experience:

1. Earphones suck because they hurt and annoy you instead of pleasing you for extended period of use. Must of us hate this because we want to enjoy music. And most of the brands just suck never being able to realize what comfort means to an extended time music listener. So we desperately want a kind of earphones that at least be able to please you not being painful for extended listening. If so, we love to have that earphones. Unfortunately, there is not a single brand that is so much comfortable, aesthetically pleasing, able to provide decent ambient sound elimination, cable quality and durability, appealing driver housing, great bass and vocal clarity, simplified control talk, solid L-shape 3.5mm jack housing and cute divider.

2. The look, feel and material of earphones just suck. Most of the midrange and high-range earphones have been using disgusted material that neither looks pleasing, nor feels awesome. We hate most of the look and feel of the earphones material. Some even smell shit. Some smell morphine and other smell smoke or kind of disgusting thing we never like. My daughter throws when I open the package of some earphones that spread motion sickness right away. Moreover, the bending, delicate wiring, fragile housing, spiteful look and terrible engineering piss me off when I pick up most of the earphones brands.

3. What drives me crazy about most of the earphones including Boss, Sony, Monster, Klipsch, Shure, Ultimate Ears, Synthesizer and you name, is their control talk design. I like to throw a hammer over the engineers that designed the control talk button in most of the low to mid to high range earphones. The technology has taken a leap and their workaround is still based on century back concept of architecture and design. None of the makers has any technology and engineering visionary that can apply an art in the control talk or the divider angle of the earphones. When I touch most of the earphones' control talk button, I like to curse at their stupid brain that even can't think an inch better for design, feel, navigation, and access and aesthetic beauty of the product. The same thing applies for the cable, coil, housing and jack. They all look and feel horrible.

4. In respect to sound clarity, distinct vocals, appealing melody, pleasing treble, magnetizing bass and infused guitar and electronics, most of the earphones suck, just suck.

5. I don't believe why technology, art, engineering, beauty, comfort and durability can't go together. These sucky earphones are looting our 100s of dollars and offering just shit. Something like this: Boos has not great bot good feel of cable though but lacks sound isolation horribly, torchers the engineering of earpiece and raises the house par your ear, abuses the art of L shape jack housing mercilessly and dumps architectural appeal to the ditch.

6. Look into Klipsch that keep designing most of the earphones look like a dick abusing genitals into the ears and embarrassing its users and admirers who have stories how quickly they pack up the earphones back into their pockets every single use. The same applies while using them hiding between fingers and quickly inserting into the ears so that nobody notices what shape is getting pushed into the ears. Kind of naked to shamelessness.

7. When we use Monster, seems like they absolutely don't care about piece of engineering and architecture for fragile cable and rough ear buds that is why they just throw thoughtless cables, and cheap buds that feels like you are pushing wooden craps into your ear canal in about 10 minutes of use. I have no idea whether they least learn about the physics and anatomy of ear canal and its sensitivity, resistance and bearing capacity. Seems like most of the earpiece makers think for tormenting eardrum. They want to beat our eardrums and beat shit out of them as much as possible; it's harassing in nature!

8. The suckiest part of the earphone makers is multi-driver unhealthy competition. I recently happened to purchase Sony XBA-4IP. Was expecting greater quality due to hyped 4 drivers claiming balanced amateur delivery and noise attenuation, comfort and clarity. What best I found in this piece of shit is only the carrying case, which is cute but not innovative.

9. Not only Sony, seems like almost all makers have lost their taste in innovation that is inherited with the best practiced engineering, craftsmanship, art, science & technology, comfort, simplicity and design in one simple package. One brand gives good cable but loses the rest. The other brand gives bass and the rests will come as pieces of whole stinking shit. Some master in vocals and not a damn thought about artistic beauty and sensitivity of ear.

10. I'm sick of these makers: none of them make a beautiful piece of earphones that is elegant in use, comfortable to wear, loving in touch, brilliant in look, strong in construction, light in weight, artistic in design and that could appeal you to get one and listen songs of your favorite selections for hours and hours very comfortably, even not knowing your ears have something into them.

So what could make the best earphone pieces that would be pure and appealing? I believe that if the following ideas are mixed in one simple package, you goanna have the best earphones ever:

1. Give me the control talk size as exactly as of standard iPhone 4S earphone piece and the length of the talk button between mouth and chin. Any length par will have direct impact to call quality, noise cancellation and wind isolation.

2. Give me the earpieces with the comfort of Klipsch but improving it to completely isolate ambient surrounding sound (and omitting the dick-like shape at the housing, just a suggestion to Klpsch).

3. Give me the driver housing like Sennheiser IE80 but modifying it with the omission of sound adjustment slot and converting it into the curved-angle triangular shape.

4. Give me the cable just like SkullCandy offers tangle free cable even to its cheapest pieces but offering elegantly bright, shiny and soothing color that is totally contrast to the existing color and would stand alone and giving the feel of lust of cable somehow like of Boss MIE2i but mastering it to the next level.

5. Give me 3.5mm jack housing quality like of Monster iBeats but modifying it into L-shape with the same elegance, brilliance, size, material and finish. Believe or not, it feels like jewel when you hide the cable and 3.5mm jack only to expose the iBeats jack housing.

6. Give me the bass improving over Boss MIE2(i) and just above iBeats but soothing like Klipsch does in most earpieces.

7. Give me the clarity like Sony XBA-4 but improving it not to dominate bass neither struggling separation.

8. Give me the case like Sony XBA-4IP but with belt mount or built-in pocket clip improving it to offering an elegant exterior slot that circles round the case mounting the cable and residing the jack and earpiece parts only inside the case, i.e. exposing the cable. The case flap would flip open and close with magnetizer. Obviously genuine leather. Not larger than Sony's contemporary case. Have a look into it if you happened to find one somewhere. There's not another earphone case coming close to Sony's case offered with XBA-4IP.

9. Give me the cable replacement option offered such as Shure does but improving the detachable cable with Monster iBeats 3.5mm jack housing like strength offering next level of secure Snap-On magnetized edges that eliminates visibility of attachment line or ring and saves your cable on accidental knock. This part would be constructed with superior engineering craftsmanship and architectural engineering.

10. Give me the earpiece in such a package that even an 8-year-old kid becomes happy to open it from the box. Such simple packaging with a decent box just like the package of an iPhone 4S: tear the thin plastic membrane and open the box as if it is designed for you exclusively to have extensive flavor of opening the package making you feel special and offering to believe that the thing inside is so much better if the housing itself it that alluring. Most of the earphones packages just suck, frustrate and even make the feel of having the purchase worse. At times I was destroying the whole case and earphones when Boss and Klipsch shipped. These guys have designed the package as if somebody would rob them tearing the case just in front of them and so had to resist. If I were the CEO of either of the companies, I would fire the designers of the case right away or destroy their concept taking shit out of them over their design briefing meetings.

If I am offered an earphone package with the mentioned 10 commandment in earphone Design, Art, Engineering, Architecture, Science and Craftsmanship brought together under one visionary principal, I would say: Yes, that is it! We all would love to hear music for hours again. And it would make the dent in the Universe. And you will rule if you are able to make such.

Remember, this review is only based on the earphones I purchased for my own use. Nobody sent me any piece for review neither they are for free. I spent almost thousand dollars in buying these pieces of shit, some more, which are not even worth mentioning here. And none of them please me. I just hate them for many reasons and some I listed here for reference in wanting one that I would love to.

Amazon asked me to write review about Boss MIE2i, but what should I write since I only hate any that I can have access too. Nothing is any better. Every earphone I have used since 2005 suck from this or that reason. Each maker seems focusing only at one area missing the whole package we are obliged to pay for.

When a consumer buys a TV, s/he doesn't only pay for Screen size, but also pays for the design, casing, corners, stand, base line, side line, resolution, contrast, clarity, features, technology, sound, remote, and so much more as a whole package. When all come in an elegant way to my observation, we say: Yes, this is it. Same thing applies for earphones.

What I have found about earphones is, the makers are throwing a lot of talk about the capacity and deliverability of the drivers they have used and talking about how comfortable they are or how much noise they can cancel. That's bullshit. Give us a package that sounds great, looks, feels, lasts, shines, and exists with us great. Talk about cable quality and lavishness on it. Talk about talk button exclusively. Talk about housing and its fit as if on debate. Talk about bass realistically. Talk about vocal and clarity distinctively. Talk about materials you have used in shaping 3.5mm jack and housing. Talk about invisible wire lines you have used and their capacity in delivering lossless sound waves into the drivers. Talk about since, engineering and art involved. Talk about the case that is specifically designed for the product for specific reasons and benefits to the user. Boss throws a case that fits most of the phones including iPhone - what an unwise thought! Klipsch throws a case made of tin like cheap material that doesn't only look cheap, feels extremely humiliating when you observe it - a brand of chewing gum ships in the same kind of box and a brand of chewing tobacco from India is infamous shipping on such case. I would like to spit on it and flush into toilet as many times I receive them.

If you are a music lover and care aesthetic beauty and art in the material you use, earphone makers have a greater challenge to accomplish which none of them will be sure daring to set forth. The only reason is that none of them has a visionary fellow that could render ideas into a beautiful and useful simple package we all are thriving to have for years. Until then, though I hate Boss MIE2i, I will be using it replacing with Sony or Klipsch or iSport occasionally.

top_profane.text:
In 1964, Walt Disney Pictures, under the directorship of Robert Stevenson, released what became a timeless musical fantasy called "Mary Poppins". Starring Julie Andrews as the mysterious & magical Mary Poppins, the film is a fictional account of a dysfunctional family living in London circa 1910. The father, George W. Banks (David Tomlinson, who played Emelius Browne in "Bedknobs and Broomsticks" in 1971), is obsessed with his job at a prestigious Fidelity Fiduciary Bank and with maintaining a professional atmosphere at all times at home. The mother, Winifred Banks (Glynis Johns, whose film career began in 1938), is preoccupied with demonstrating as a suffragette and maintaining a clean home. Their young children, Jane (Karen Dotrice) and Michael (Matthew Garber, who died at the young age of 21 in 1977), are allowed minimal time with their parents, who prefer to have a nanny look after and raise their children for them. Unfortunately, Jane and Michael rarely like any of their nannies, who regularly quit after the children do something mischievous to each of them. Also working for Mr. & Mrs. Banks are the maid Ellen (Hermione Baddeley, who played Mrs. Cratchit in the 1951 classic "Scrooge") and the cook Mrs. Brill (Reta Shaw, who played in "Pollyanna" in 1960 and "The Ghost & Mr. Chicken" in 1966), neither of which have the time or the desire to look after Jane and Michael.

Following the departure of yet another nanny, George decides to hire an appropriate nanny himself. Jane and Michael write their own advertisement for a nanny, but George regards their innocent description as rubbish as he intends to find a nanny that will uphold his every professional expectation. Mysteriously, the children's advertisement, that George tossed out, comes to Mary Poppins. When a very lengthy line of perspective nannies are seen at the Banks' front door early one morning, all of them are blown away by a strong wind. Coming down from the sky via an umbrella is the magical Mary Poppins. Expecting a rush of perspective nannies to race through the door, only Mary Poppins waits to enter the Banks' home. George interviews Mary, but he is quickly confused by her wit and unexpectedly hires her. From that point on, nothing is quite the same in the Banks home and the children get a nanny that not only fulfills their expectations, but becomes something much more to them. Along the way, the children are also introduced to several interesting people, including the handyman Bert (Dick Van Dyke, who starred in his own 1961 TV show and starred in "Chitty Chitty Bang Bang"), Uncle Albert (Ed Wynn, who also played the toy-maker in "Babes in Toyland" in 1961) and the bird woman (Jane Darwell).

One of the most important aspects of "Mary Poppins" that transformed it into a timeless classic is its wonderful music as composed by Richard M. Sherman and Robert B. Sherman, as well as sung by its original cast. My ratings for each of the songs on the first disk of this wonderful two-CD soundtrack are listed below:

Disk #1:

1. "Buena Vista" (4.5, instrumental).

2. "Overture" (5, instrumental).

3. "One Man Band" (5+, Dick Van Dyke). Bert as a one man-band.

4. "Sister Suffragette" (4.5/5, Glynis John). Winifred calling for the rights of women to be able to vote.

5. "The Life I Lead" (5/5, David Tomlinson). George describing his life and what a British home should be.

6. "The Perfect Nanny" (4.5/5, Karen Dotrice & Matthew Garber). Jane & Michael telling what they want in a nanny to their parents.

7. "Air Mail/Admiral Boom/The Not-So-Perfect Nannies/Mary Poppins Arrives" (5, instrumental).

8. "A Spoonful of Sugar" (5+/5, Julie Andrews). Mary encouraging the children to clean their rooms upon her arrival.

9. "Pavement Artist (Chim Chim Cher-ee)" (5/5, Dick Van Dyke). Bert's carefree life.

10. "Jolly Holiday" (4.5/5, Dick Van Dyke). Bert describing how wonderful it is to be with Mary Poppins again within the sidewalk chalk painting.

11. "Jolly Holiday (Reprise)" (4, instrumental).

12. "Penguin Dance" (4.5, instrumental).

13. "The Carousel Horses" (4.5, instrumental).

14. "Supercalifragilisticexpialidocious" (5+/5, Julie Andrews & Dick Van Dyke). A word you use when you don't know what else to say.

15. "Pavement Artist (Reprise)" (4.5, instrumental).

16. "Stay Awake" (5/5, Julie Andrews). A lullaby to Jane and Michael.

17. "Trouble At Uncle Albert's" (4.5, instrumental).

18. "I Love To Laugh" (4.5/5, Julie Andrews, Dick Van Dyke, Ed Wynn). Uncle Albert is visited who is suffering from incurable laughter.

19. "A British Bank (The Life I Lead") (5/5, David Tomlinson & Julie Andrews). Mary telling George that he'll be taking the children to the bank with him.

20. "Feed the Birds (Tuppence a Bag)" (5+/5, Julie Andrews). Mary describing the life of a poor woman who enjoys feeding birds.

21. "Father's Footsteps" (5, instrumental).

22. "Fidelity Fiduciary Bank" (5/5, Dick Van Dyke and David Tomlinson) Mr. Dawes Sr. (also played by Dick Van Dyke), the CEO of George's bank, sings praises to the British banking system with George to Jane and Michael.

23. "Panic At The Bank" (5, instrumental).

24. "Chim Chim Cher-ee/March Over The Rooftops" (5+/5, Dick Van Dyke). Bert describing the carefree life of chimney sweep.

25. "Step in Time" (4/5, Dick Van Dyke). Song and dance upon the sooty rooftops of London.

26. "A Man Has Dreams (The Life I Lead/A Spoonful Of Sugar)" (4.5/5 Dick Van Dyke and David Tomlinson) George singing his woes to Bert.

27. "Mr. Banks Is Discharged" (4.5, instrumental).

28. "Let's Go Fly a Kite" (4.5/5, David Tomlinson and Glynis Johns). George & Winifred spending time with Jane and Michael.

The bonus material on Disk #2 is very interesting to listen to. Overall, I rate the two-CD enhanced soundtrack of "Mary Poppins" with a resounding 5 out of 5 stars and highly recommend it.
top_profane.title: An Album that's Supercalifragilisticexpialidocious!

top_profane.text:
I listened to the audiobook read by Milton Bagby that's 8 hours and 38 minutes long. The last paragraph of this review focuses on that production specifically.

4 stars. A great, if a tad general/folksy, PTO tanker memoir and a great memoir portraying the overall military experience in the Pacific.

Robert Dick was a tank driver in the 763rd Tank Battalion during World War 2. He saw action on Leyte in 1944 and later on Okinawa in 1945 after an extensive experience in the US Army.

There is a lot on Dick's pre-combat experience in the infantry before he joins an armored unit and ships out to the Pacific. Several anecdotes commonly seen in memoirs from soldiers who were in the army before/just around the attack on Pearl Harbor (poorly organized into bizarre guard duties). Dick finds himself in charge of checkpoints at important spots on the West Coast and details several charming stories of soldier antics on this topic.

Eventually Dick joins the 763rd Tank Battalion and becomes a driver of an American Sherman tank (tank number 60 and in, IIRC, C Company, leading to the Cutthroats nickname coinage). He is extensive in describing his combat role and the functions and tech of the tanks and this is presented in an interesting way. Here Dick does a great job of describing the mechanics and other technical aspects of armored warfare that is easily digestible.

As a tank driver, Dick is more of a spectator when it comes to combat action. He's in several souvenirs hunts where he does explicitly describe firing his Thompson Sub-Machine gun in a skirmish with the Japanese on Leyte and this is described forthrightly, if not in a compelling manner akin to other PTO combat memoirs. However this is a small part of the overall combat experience Dick undergoes. Instead, Dick details the issues with driving a tank in the very different landscapes between Leyte (a jungle-thick mess of muddy roads) and Okinawa (a smorgasbord of Japanese-held hills and valleys) and though eye opening, it's not presented in such a raw or energetic manner.

Another reviewer itemizes this memoir to a sit down with Dick at a bar, hearing him relate his war experience over a couple of a beers. This is a perfect description of the books' flavor - Dick isn't coy on the details, nor is the language (though not at all frequent) sanitized. But the delivery is very laid back and features some good humor throughout. I read lots of war memoirs and rate them mostly based on the author's willingness to detail their own personal experience in combat and writing ability. Dick is willing to divulge his own contribution to the effort, though limited with respect to his combat as a tank driver, and his writing is very approachable.

I can see this book as a primary source to a tanker-version of THE PACIFIC, it touches on all the right topics in an armored combatants' experience. So because of this and all the other positive aspects of the memoir (with the drawbacks mentioned) I give this one a 4-star rating. This is a pretty good rating on my scale and this book is definitely one of the first ones I would mention when describing good all-around PTO memoirs.

Milton Bagby reads this account and does so with the exact delivery I'd imagine Dick was going for (sharing a few beers at the bar, remember). Bagby is an older narrator and narrates in a folksy and approachable manner. I usually think combat accounts should be read by younger narrators (or in a younger-style?) but this works very well with CUTTHROATS, so I have no complaints. I also listened to [[ASIN:B009EE1EL2 Fighting with the Filthy Thirteen: The World War II Story of Jack Womer-Ranger and Paratrooper]] by Jack Womer and Steve Devito, narrated by John Allen Nelson. For the longest time I was ready to write a review with the assumption that these two books shared a narrator, but after doubling checking, they don't! I still think that if you liked this narration then you'd like Nelson's, though.
top_profane.title: A thoroughly enjoyable account of tank warfare in the Pacific

Holy [Crap]! What in the [HECK] just happened!?!? I........, I don't even know what to say right now, [crap]! Just give me a second, I need to pick my [butt] up off the ground and pull my heart out of Kitten Jackson's fist real quick and figure out what the [heck] I just witnessed! What in the [heck] was THAT?!?

Alright, now. I guess I should tell you a bit about the story, yeah, we'll start there. Maybe once we get the ball rolling I'll shake off some of the fear that's frozen me stupid. Sheesus, I swear!

This is a seriously psychotic and [screwed,] [SCREWED] up love story. This story made me question my morals (which, [heck] they already were pretty few and far between) and my self beliefs! And, I'm pretty [dang] sure I'm going to hell for even calling it a love story, but I really don't give a [crap,] 'cause that's what it is. It's wrong, so [dang] wrong. I can't even tell you how wrong, and I loved it! I tore through this [crap] like a fiend and loved every second of it!!!

This bat [crap] crazy, masochistic, and totally [messed] up love story belongs to Abbie and Greg, it isn't a love story that I envy, or one that I'd wish on anyone, however, it is theirs and theirs alone.
Their story isn't for the faint at heart, btw, if you hadn't gathered that from all the rambling that occurred up there. ^

We begin the story with Greg, he has been on a hamster wheel, just going through the motions over the last 15 years. And he is getting sick and [dang] tired of it.
Greg's character is absolutely fascinating to me. His character has the ability to simultaneously make you cringe, twist your face in disgust, and, yes I'm actually fixin' to [freakin] saying this, warm your heart and feel sympathy and compassion for him. You want to fix him so bad. You know he wants to be fixed, he doesn't like what he is, [heck] has tried to, no he DID, run from it before. He has more issues than half the psych ward down town. However his main issues begin with Sex. In order for him to become aroused, he has for the last 15 years, had to simulate rape.

Society tells us that this is wrong, and if you need to hurt, or rape to reach orgasm, then you should probably see the nearest available psychiatrist. However! Yes, I'm defending him! In some circles, he would be called a Sadist, and if he were to stay in said circles, he'd probably be a bad a*s. Anywho, back to the point. As the story continues, we find out that the only woman he ever loved Abbie, was his first, first victim, and his "first" first.

He recently found her again through Facebook and he has contacted her. He sees she isn't in a relationship, and decides he is ready to go back home. Johnny Moretti ran away from home 15 years ago after he drunkenly lost his [crap] and raped the love of his life and best friend. Yep, he knew he had totally [messed] up, so he took off, gathered as much cash as he could, some clothes, and just took off. Johnny Moretti was supposed to have died that night, died, dead......gone. Greg Parker is a new man, in a new town. And he is ready to make something of himself.

Now, 15 yrs later, Greg wants to go home, he wants back in Abbie's life. He looks NOTHING like he did the last time Abbie saw him, he knows she'll never recognize him. And he knows that she is the only woman for him, and with Abbie he can have the life he always wanted, he would never hurt her again. He loves her, how could he? He knows he can make it right this time! So he throws out the bait, sends Abbie a few messages thru Facebook, letting her know he's gonna be in town on business and that he may stop by her coffee shop. Plane tickets bought, he packs, he is so excited to be with Abbie again.......

Abbie has lived in Pensacola her whole life. After her grandmother passed away a few years back she inherits her home and coffee shop. She renovates both, and directs the coffee shop in a new and up to date direction. She has great friends, and life may have just gotten a little better. The hottie from FB, he said they'd met before, but she doesn't recall it, and with him being as fine as he is, she finds that odd. He would've definitely stuck out in her memory, anyway, he just sent her a message, he'll be in town on business and will probably stop by the shop!

When Abbie and Greg come together the chemistry is off the charts! She has never felt this way about any man! Abbie has a hard time keeping her head on straight half the time. And Greg knows he isn't leaving, he and Abbie are too right for each other, she soothes the beast in him, and for the first time in his life, he is able to bring himself relief without violent thoughts. Finally, he can have the life he was supposed to have.

Then things begin to get tricky, Greg's best friend Mark and his girlfriend turn up murdered. While Greg is in New York helping the family and grieving his best friend, Abbie's ex boyfriend attacks her, beating the [crap] out of her and tries to rape her. Greg gets both business and personal affairs settled in New York and heads back to help Abbie out after the attack. Besides a bruised and battered Abbie, their relationship begins to grow pretty quickly, he has told her he plans to move to Pensacola and she is out of this world excited!

How can a woman heal a broken man over night? Fix all of his issues, 15 yr old issues, just by being with him, around him, and loving him. We know he's Keeping Secrets, and Abbie has a few of her own, so when the [crap] hits the fan, when the lid is ripped off of Pandora's box, is it even possible for Abbie to listen, much less forgive Greg? How long will Greg keep his beast on a leash, it's getting harder and harder to hold it back. Is everything as it seems? Has the light been shed on all things and shown to the reader?
Ha, well [heck] I'm not telling ya! I will say, only because I love you, that when you are on Amazon/Smashwords or at your neighborhood book store, do yourself a favor and pick up BOTH Keeping Secrets I & II, because the only thing that got me through writing this review is that I know if I hurry this [crap] up, I can go diving off into Keeping Secrets II: No More Skeletons! Because the cliffhanger in book one left me screaming, raving, and ranting like, well, like I did up there at the beginning of my review. Yep, just like that, lmao.

Keeping Secrets gets 5 -The most ugly, masochist, [messed] up love story of all time-- Kimmi stars! Those are always the best, 'cause those type of stars make ya know that you may have finished this story, but it's not finished with you.................

top_profane.text:
4.2 stars!

As much as I like Crushed, I feel that Vagabond was deeper—in both theme and plot. It was pretty cool that Vagabond explores issues of racism, and of racial and biracial identities. Gosh, yes, that “Where are you really from?” question is terrible. I am frankly baffled why so many people don’t know how offensive this question is to POC (people of color) individuals. It’s much rarer for a white person to be asked about their “country of origin” or “ethnic roots”! Anyway, I found this aspect of Vagabond very interesting, especially as I’m Chinese myself. Race is a sensitive but also necessary discussion topic.

Nevertheless, I’m very glad that Ludlow does know about Asians, despite living all his life (if I remember right) in a small town. I once read about a character who lived all her life in a small town in England, and had consequently never seen Asians before! Which really shocked me.

Aside from the topic of race, I also loved the other themes in Vagabond. One theme was on being a perpetual outsider, where you’re always the odd one out, never fitting in anywhere. I feel like a misfit in most settings too, for example, because I’m bi and nonbinary trans, as well as being on the aro ace spectra. It can get pretty lonely, even if you have supportive friends, and classmates and teachers who respect you. So, I would say I can relate to Dick in some way: Even if most people around you like and respect you or even find you charming, they may still see you as an outsider…Which really sucks because you just want to be one of them rather than to be this exotic, fascinating creature to be oohed and aahed at. Maybe I’m exaggerating this feeling a bit, but you get the picture.

Another intriguing theme in Vagabond, was the issue of fully planning out your life versus being very adventurous. Ludlow has the former type of lifestyle and Dick the latter. I enjoy reading romances where the heroes learn a lot from each other, which was what happened in this book. The two men seemed to have found an ideal balance of the two contrasting lifestyles by the end of the story. It’s notable, though, that unlike many other books, Vagabond doesn’t promote the utter spontaneity that Dick represented; instead, the book shows us the downsides of too much freedom and spontaneity as well, which I appreciated because I’m someone who doesn’t believe in going to extremes. Balance is key!

One last theme I loved from this book, was whether everybody needed special, meaningful moments in life; and whether some people just go through their life without ever reflecting on it. That reminded me of what Socrates said: The unexamined life is not worth living. It was interesting that Ludlow felt he was one of those “ordinary” folks who live their whole lives without thinking about the deeper meanings behind it, while Dick disagreed with Ludlow. I don’t know Ludlow’s entire backstory, but it did appear to me that Ludlow wasn’t that reflective on his life, until he met Dick. But I would imagine that being a perpetual outsider and travelling all around the world would make Dick a more thoughtful, questioning person, as Dick would be much less likely to take social norms and beliefs for granted.

More things on the book:
I adored Teensy. Her name adds to her overall cuteness. I loved reading the descriptions of her movements, especially through some vivid verbs. It was pretty funny to watch Dick and Ludlow argue about how best to take care of Teensy too.

The plot of not being sure if the other person feels anything more than physical attraction, and secretly wanting to be long-term partners but fearing that the other person wouldn’t want to be “tied down,” is a very common romance plotline, but the plot nevertheless felt tense and suspenseful in Vagabond, even though I know there will be a happy ending! So the emotional tension was good.

The protagonists, Dick and Ludlow, felt more developed and complex than Harley and Tynan (from Crushed), though the latter two aren’t flat characters either. Maybe because there was more detailed talk on Dick’s and Ludlow’s backstories and worldviews, at least from what I see.

Overall, I loved Vagabond very much. Philosophically, I think this book was the most profound out of Crushed, Vagabond, and Lost and Found.
top_profane.title: Loved the philosophical themes

top_profane.text:
I loved all the stories within Royal Harlots. If you love MC stories and strong heroines then Royal Harlots is for you.

Oh my…,Banshee is the perfect beginning to the Royal Harlots. I loved how strong willed Lil is and reading all about Lil and Cian. Ramsey did a fantastic job with Banshee and it fits perfectly with Royal Harlots which is the sister club to the Royal Bastards. I enjoyed reading how Dash pushes Peppermint out of her comfort zone and how following your heart and pushing your limits can have so much rewards. J.L. Leslie did an excellent job with Pushing the Limit and I highly recommend the Royal Harlots and reading Pushing the Limit. If you love MC romances I can also recommend J.L. Leslie’s additions to the Royal Bastards’ series with Worth the Pain and Worth the Risk. You won’t regret reading any of the stories I have recommended above. 😊🥰

top_profane.text:
Boy, oh, boy! If I knew cowboys are that hot, I would have started reading western romance a lot sooner! And Cora Seton's cowboys are nothing if not scorching, deliciously hot! Since I had the extreme luck of winning all three of the first books from her Chance Creek series, I enjoyed some cowboy lovin' at its best!

"I’m just a man, Autumn. A flawed man. But I love you more than you will ever know." - Ethan Cruz

First we have Ethan and Autumn's story(yeah, I know, I cringed at her name, too).

Ethan Cruz is screwed every way from here to next Thursday. His parents died in an accident, leaving him to pay their debts, his sister turned her back on him when he asked her for help, and the woman he thought he loved dumped him to find greener pastures. To make matters worse, one of his best friends recorded him while - in his drunken state - he listed what a cowboy's wife should be like and uploaded it on YouTube, making it look like an ad for an email order bride. Now Ethan must choose between sending Autumn Leeds, who came to Chance Creek, Montana, to be that bride, back home to New York, or keeping up the charade and see if he can have his HEA with a woman that's nothing like the materialistic females he's had in his life so far. But Autumn has a secret of her own. She's not really here to stay, but to write a juicy, humiliating article on Ethan's "email order bride" ad in order to keep her place in the magazine she's working for. Can a relationship that started on lies from both parties survive long enough for love to hold it together? And what happens when the masks fall and truth comes to light?

I'll come out and say it right now, the main plot sort of reminded me of How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days - amazing movie, btw, go watch it if for some God forsaken reason you still haven't done so. A relationship based on lies, where both parties fall in love with each other and decide to keep the charade up because they realize their lives can't be complete without the other half anymore. And while the main idea may not be new, it still was somehow even better than the movie was!

Ethan was a dream turned into a male wearing a stetson and worn-out boots, I swear! He was romantic, sweet, hard-working, and while he kept up with the initial lie that he had put up that wife ad, his morals were pretty high. Definitely a man yours truly would die to have as the daddy to her kids, no kidding! He was all for working hard to make sure his woman would miss nothing, but he didn't take away her freedom like several country bumpkins have done before. You know, the whole "me man brings bacon, you woman stay home and cook the bacon" theme that makes some women melt because they think they've found the ultimate Alpha?

You see, our dear Mr. Cruz was definitely an Alpha. But it was not in a vulgar or extremely violent and macho way. Instead he was calm - well, as calm as he could be, given the circumstances - and nice, polite even when the situation called for some much needed punches. The way he felt whenever Autumn surprised him with her good natured replies - like he never expected a female to be nice to him after the way his mother, his sister and his ex, had treated him - nearly made me cry. And while he didn't have the means, he still wanted to give her the stars just for being by his side. It was adorable!

As for Autumn... Well, it's hard to hate her, given what she had been through and the fact she really needed to keep her job. She had seen first hand that depending on a man was never going to take her anywhere in life, and she was trying to make sure she could survive on her own. She knew perfectly well why she couldn't trust men, accepted it, and didn't blame the wrong people for that. My only problem was, why keep trying to make yourself believe you're better off writing that damn article, girl, when you actually enjoy being a rancher's wife? It made no sense, the way she kept talking herself into a dilemma. That and the pregnancy thing. I get it, female intuition and all that, but even so it wasn't really believable, especially so soon after she slept with Ethan.

Concerning the other characters, Mrs. Seton has created quite a cast. I've already started the 5th book, so I'm not gonna give you any spoilers, but she seems to be a master in taking what looks like a secondary character and making them main ones. A small, warm Montana town full of potential, if you ask me, that's what her Chance Creek is. When I started this book (I mean, the first one) I immediately fell in love with Cab and Rob - and let's admit it, Ethan's prank was even harsher given the way women treated Rob afterwards - and I assure you I couldn't wait to read their stories. As for Lacey, well, I understand where she comes from, but that didn't make me like her any more. Still, since the writer makes believable turns in her characters' personalities like nobody's business, I'm certain I will come to adore her if she ever gets her own love story in the series.

"I want us to make it." - Autumn Leeds

"I don’t care about the house, or how much money you spent or anything but the fact that you’re safe and here with me. I love you. You know that, right? Tell me you know that, Claire." - Jamie Lassiter

Aaah, the second story. Well, this is going to be hard to hide, so I'll say it now to get it out of my chest - the main reason why this book set got 4 stars was the fact I rated the 2nd book with 3 stars (and that's only because the rest of the characters were in the story, otherwise the 2nd book would have been a definite DNF).

Jamie Lassiter always knew, in his heart and mind, that he wanted to marry Claire Cruz, his best friend's big sister. Unfortunately, Claire never had eyes for him - that is, until he grew up to fill those sweaty shirts and dirty jeans quite nicely. Now that he's Ethan's partner in the Cruz Guest Ranch, he's hoping he can finally pop the question he's longed to ask for a while now. But a well-timed joke that Rob pulled on Claire is wreaking havoc on his carefully organized plans - and so the natural flirt of the cowboy quartet will have to win the hardest bet ever presented to him: make Claire fall in love with him without touching or flirting with her and any other woman in six weeks, or pay for her worldwide cruise himself.

I don't have much to say for Jamie. It's true that he was a nice guy, and that his intentions for Claire were really sweet, but... You have to see it the way I do in order to understand my dilemma. Ethan is the tortured one, Rob the misunderstood clown/jokester, and Cab the quiet, steady force. Jamie, other than his persistent love for Claire, his model-like looks, and his flirting, isn't all that distinguishing when he stands next to his friends, you know what I'm saying? If he was alone in the page, sure, he had my full attenion, but when one or more of his pals were with him, then the spotlight easily transferred to them even if they weren't the ones doing the talking. He was just a handsome so-and-so good guy, but nothing to write home about. Still, I have to admit I loved and completely understood his careful planning. Marriage and career are important in a person's life. It's only natural that he was slow on each step and thought it through.

Aaand, it's about we all talked about the damn elephant in the room. The really stinky one that nearly ruined this book - and the 3rd book, too, simply by being present, geez! That's right, I'm talking about Claire. I hate her. I hated her when she made her debut appearance in Ethan's story, and I hated her even more as Jamie's story progressed. As a matter of fact, with Rob's story, she even outshines Lacey in bitchiness! At least Lacey realized her problem and went to some kind of therapy - Claire, on the other hand, not only knew her problems, but she also refused to do something about them even when she kept hurting those around her! Seriously, for one, she fell in love with her mother's lover of all people - God, the woman was stupid way before she reached adulthood! She goes around acting like the poor victim because mommy dearest didn't try to understand or help her (or it could be because she took her "dream guy", but whatever), yet she never even tried to help her own little brother when he was obviously in need of some help. The only thing she could see was her own hatred and emotional distress that the ranch brought to her - let's of course forget the fact Ethan considered it his home, because, screw you, she's Claire Cruz, she calls dibs in misery and comfort, duh! Rose was right, she was too busy caring only for her skinny little ass to give a damn about others and the way they tried their hardest to show her she was loved. I pitied poor Jamie throughout the whole ordeal. Every little smile he offered to her or any other female counted as flirting in her book, and the fact she never knew what she wanted was definitely his fault, am I right? Oh, wait... NOT! And then, when he finally made her understand his love was for real, she had to go and keep making his life a personal hell. Had I been in the guy's shoes, I would have stopped trying long ago!

Not that Jamie was perfect in morals. Both were in fault, actually. She didn't spare a second glance when he was young (enlighten me a bit on this one... so, in other words, she didn't care for Jamie till he became a hot piece of ass - after all, she did say she was looking for a boyfriend she could brag about like he was a pair of earrings. niiice, such deep and honest thinking you've got going there, Claire...), and he kept sleeping around till she was ready to give in to his charms. This was seriously messed up from the beginning, and, as shown in the 3rd book (which I will come to very shortly), it's a miracle they haven't divorced yet.

What saved the second story was the fact that we got to know one more dirty secret of Aria's - good Lord, the woman had more sides than an unfolded Rubik's Cube! That and Morgan. I seriously loved Morgan. Too bad she has to endure Claire, poor girl deserved better than that...

(by the way, in case you haven't noticed, I tend to start each review with one declaration of love, and end it with another. any wonder why there was no declaration on the end of this one? oh, that's right, because Miss Badass Bitch can't bring herself to say the words out loud, no, Jamie has to guess them from her thoughts and actions! unless I managed to somehow miss them, I don't know...)

"I think I love you." - Morgan Tate

Oh, Rob and Morgan... My favorite of the first three pairings! I seriously loved them both as individuals, and as a couple!

Rob Matheson is the prankster of the cowboy quartet. The youngest of four sons, he's learnt his lesson all too well - to survive, you either do it with your fists, or with your wits. And Rob definitely has wit in his arsenal, but he's rarely given the chance to prove he can do anything worthwhile and be responsible. So when his father, Holt Matheson, suggests a contest between his fous sons - Jake, Ned, Luke and Rob - to get them married with the promise of land to the first one that manages the feat, Rob immediately leaps at the opportunity to get out of his father's control and his brothers' bullying behavior. But where to find a bride willing to tie the knot with him? Morgan Tate, Claire and Ethan's half-sister, seems quite eager to accept, and he's had her in his sights ever since he met her, so promising her a winery should not be a big deal, right? Still, even after they come to a mutual agreement which soon blossoms into love, can they stick to their plans for the future against everyone determined to stop them?

I think I said it in Ethan's story, already: I love Rob! I knew he was terribly misunderstood - most pranksters are. And now that I know the reason behind all those jokes of his, well, that made me like him even more. Considering the way his own family treated him, he could have turned out a lot worse. He was so intelligent, sensitive, artistic and nerdy, and he was trying to hide it all because everyone around him kept questioning his masculinity if he so much as tried to give his dreams a second thought. Plus, he was much more mature than anyone could have guessed. It was wonderful to know that Morgan could see past that and ignore everyone else's judgement, that she saw him for who he was and could be, and that she fully supported him.

Morgan was an equally delightful main heroine. Finally someone who didn't lie, who adored her husband-to-be and trusted him completely. Someone who had his back 100%, against all odds. She didn't care what others said about him, and she let him do his thing, knowing he would prevail in the end. That's a true and honest relationship, if I ever saw one. The fact she was the shame-child for both of her parents, and was almost always treated like trash from Claire only served to show what a wonderful woman she really was - like Rob, she could have ended up being a totally hateful person, but she didn't.

Rob's relationship with Morgan was one of the most beautiful romantic relationships I have ever read! They started out as partners in the same scheme, who knew that they were in it not only for the money and the land, but also because they respected each other and were equally attracted to the other party. They were honest with their feelings, and didn't fear telling their significant other their thoughts. They had each other's back, and they generally supported and trusted their lover fully. Not once did they have a fight during the book, and while they started out slowly, their love was obvious even before they said the words out loud. It was literally them against the rest of the world - particularly, Rob's family and Morgan's sister.

While this book rated in my head as a 5-star one - yes, I liked it that much - there were a few things that ruined my mood - though none of them brought the rating down. One, as you can guess it, is Claire. Oh, Claire, how I love to hate you. I guess you decided to be promoted from Badass Bitch to Crying Bitch - but a bitch all the same, hun, make no mistake! Well, in the last book, little Claire's problem was that she had a bone to pick with her dead mother. At this book, she takes it a step further and barks at everyone around her, because she's not sure she wants to become a mother at the fear of becoming like Aria. Of course, once again, it's all Jamie's fault for wanting to start a family...
(where the heck is that wall to bang my head on when I need it, damn it?!)

Worst of all was, Jamie actually became such a whimp in this book, he blindly accepted it all, blamed himself for his - constantly bitching - bride, and then accusing others - mainly Rob and Morgan - for good measure. It didn't matter that what Claire was doing was threatening to destroy her sister's chance - and her husband's best friend - at a home and a life, Claire had no trouble kicking them to the curb by turning the guilt flashlight on them, and Jamie was all in for assisting her in the task. Should we feel sympathetic because of their condition? No, I don't think so. Rob and Morgan were not the ones to blame for what was going wrong in their marriage, and they had been everyone's scapegoat for far too long to deserve any of this crap.
(I say we all go kill Claire and then match Jamie with a woman that actually has a heart - or, well, a functioning brain. Who's with me?) And don't get me started on the fact they were actually horrified and wanted Rob and Morgan back on the Cruz Ranch when things got rough. I expected this from Claire, but really, seeing Jamie so ruined by her, I was disappointed. I guess they shall remain the couple I hate till the end of the series.

Another problem - well, not really, just a minor one - Ned and his father, Holt. Rob's family has some serious issues, I tell you. For one, Holt reminded me of a mannerless Daniel MacGregor in his bossy ways and his attempts to marry his sons off and see grandkids. But I came to sort of understand him and feel somewhat sympathetic for his condition by the end of the book. Still, he has a long way to go before becoming a good enough father in my eyes. As for Ned... That damn brute better learn some manners by the time it's his turn to find love (I saw Jake's book on NetGalley, so I have no doubt the rest of the Matheson boys will have their own books, too). In fact, I'm kinda wishing he finds a scholar, someone bookish and nerdy and quiet, so that the loud, rude idiot can learn his lesson in treating others with respect and some gentleness. It would certainly be interesting to see.

"I know I love you." - Rob Matheson

All in all, I loved Cora Seton's writing voice. I'm a major fan for good ol' fashioned courting, and while it wasn't that obvious, all three of the first heroes were doing their best in wooing the women that held their hearts. They were sweet and seriously adorable, and I swear to God, I'm gonna try my best to get my hands on the rest of the books! Can't wait to find out about Carl and other characters of Chance Creek, too. Mark my words, this series should be a must-read in the romance genre!

***I was given a review copy from a LibraryThing Member Giveaway in exchange for an honest review. The opinion stated in this review is solely mine, and no compensation was given or taken to alter it.***
top_profane.title: Find me a cowboy, and fast!

top_profane.text:
This book was quite a fun read. A joyful romp with a chubby journalist girl and her hot, nearly bi-sexual girlfriend. Chubby is out for the scoop of a lifetime. Vampires are killing prostitutes in L.A., and this frisky Scooby-Doo duo are the only ones who can solve the case!

This book reminds me a lot of Janet Evanovich's One for the Money, in it's tone and pace. The witty banter, and crazy girl antics are fun to read. But, it's a bit shallow.

I mean, of course it's shallow. It's not really meant to be anything else, is it? It's just written to be a fun book. No extra thinking required, right?

Unlike Janet Evanovich's books, the characters in this book are even more empty. I mean, why is Chubby such a bitch? She's not written like she's had a rough life. Nobody really treats her like a fat girl. Guys even flirt with her. So, why's she such a bitch?

Her friend Tatum is a slut, I get that. The world needs sluts, and they don't need to be any deeper than they are. Sluts are cardboard characters. But, it would have been a more interesting story if she wasn't really a slut. Like, maybe she had a reason for what she was doing. Maybe she had daddy issues, who the hell knows. But, dagnabbit, in a book as long as this one is, 300 some pages, I really want more depth from a story.

And this story doesn't even merit 300 pages. It's a short story at best. Maybe 20 pages or so. I found myself going, dagnabbit man, get to the good stuff already. Find the bad guys, have a shoot out, do something to make me actually care about this stupid story.

I mean, so vampires are killing whores. So-bloody-what. Who the frick cares? Why is this even a story? They keep toying with the idea that: no, vampires aren't real. It's not really vampires, it's probably some losers that are just playing with the whole vampire lifestyle. Sucking blood, and all that bullshiat. But, again, who the bloody hell cares?

It's just not a very interesting story. The only reason to read this book, is because you love the witty banter between these two Scooby-Doo girls. And, I'll grant you that it's still a fun book to read. The problem is, the villain in the story isn't revealed until the very end. So, at no time is the reader going: Yea! Get that bastard. Just shoot him in the face! Because, there's no bastard to shoot at until the very end.

So, there's just nothing to cheer for. Nothing in the story gives the reader a reason to give a bloody damn. It just drags us on with endless girly talk and wanna-be detective meandering. I don't bloody care. Give me a mother-clucking villain to hate. Give me a bloody good reason to keep reading, to see that justice is done.

Or just shut up and suck some dick, you stupid fat whore. Because at least I would care about the ending. At least I would want to know: did he shoot it all over her face? Get it in her hair? Oh damn, he got it in her eye! Nice. See, even sucking cock has a better plot than this bloody book.

I received a free copy of this book for an honest review.
top_profane.title: The Scene - is full of bimbos and bubblegum

top_profane.text:
"Visibly Broken" , absolutely, it nearly broke me, be warned this story is on the dark side.
With warnings aside I still give this one a solid three and a half stars. Jason Stanley, son of the mayor has grown up with all sorts of opportunities , but oppurtunities mean nothing when your abusive father uses you as his fighting bag. Now a grown man and living with his girlfreind Missy Jason is determined to break the cycle of abuse. Jason" Cobra" Stanley has turned to MMA fighting to help release his steam and help to keep the abuse out of his home life , which for the most part he succeeds . Most part being the operative words,because his girlfriend Missy well , she just is trouble on two legs, Missy is aware of Jason's short fuse and his desire to never touch her I anger but when she does not get her way she purposely pushes his buttons , leading to a scene or two were Jason's upbringing comes in to play. On one such night Jason storms out to a underground fight that lands him in the hospital, badly beaten, unconscious and alone . He awakes to a beautiful blonde angel, Lorraine Bosch sitting at his bedside , but when they start pushing for information , his name, address and insurance he beats a hasty retreat, pain of no pain and also makes the decision that after three years he and Missy are over, they are just to toxic for one another to spite loving her .
Jason, can not get his angel from the hospital " Lo " out of his mind , but he is no good for someone like her , so when his frustration level reaches a high he heads to the Lions Den A local BDSM club he belongs to in hopes of some play time. Jason is not so much into the BDSM aspect as he isinto the total control, however he finds it easy to loose his own Control when a women with Dark hair and similar eyes and facial features to his " Angel" shows up. Sex against a wall is suddenly on the menu for the evening, but this beauty is still distracted and watching another man while having sex with him. When the women takes off Jason follows intent on giving her a piece of his mind only to get a surprise, the dark haired beauty turns out to be his Blonde angel in disguise .
"Lo" has just as many issues as him, if not more. Lorraine has had tragedy hit close to home, one that has her living in fear most of the time , except when she is taking on the alter ego of her twin sister Heidi. Everything about this women screams for Jason to protect her, be with her, so much so that after a incident at her home he moves her in with him. He tells her she should run from him, that he is no good, but Lo insist he will never hurt her and actually he is overprotective of her.
Together these two become a support system for each other , Lorraine is a calming influence on Jason, amongst other changes he wonders about.
"When the hell did I become a cat man? I have lots of love for pussy. I love a variety of pussy: dark pussy, light pussy, even multicolored, tattooed pussy. I have had smooth pussy and fuzzy pussy, but never did I think I would get up in the morning and worry about feeding pussy rather than feasting on pussy."<\b>

When it comes to Lo, Jason will do anything for her and the same can be said about Lorraine for Jason.
Though this story starts out dark and the mystery they unravel for Lorraine is dark and sad, the story line holds your interest , the sexy times abound and the mystery is solved all leading up to an HEA.
Arc Graciuosly provided by Netgalley for an honest review.

top_rant.text:
I learned the hard way from my mistakes that a cheap battery can cost you way more than its worth..Wish I would have never bought this cheap battery even though worked good for 3 flights with Morpiolot battery before it crashed my drone.. This Battery Cost me wrecking my drone losing all power and dropping out of air! Everyone needs to be very aware of what you risk buying this battery and I'm not saying it isn't a good battery or deal, just in my case is a very very poor investment. I want to inform everyone to watch out for certain issues if you buy this battery so you don't have the same problems I did. Because normally the drone will return to home or Auto land but if you get a defect like I got in battery none of this works and the drone just falls out of air and Morpilot don't care and address any of it so far in my emails as of 8-15-2016
This is my story and its long so if you don't have time, dont' read it.
Extremely upset about the quality control on the battery I received. I noticed one of the gold contacts was very slightly crimped when received the battery. This was where I should of immediately sent it back for a full refund! So anyone else out there, dont' do this and use battery if arrives or is hard to put on. I don't want it to cost you your drone like it did me. My mistake. I thought that is wasn't super bad crimp or bend so though would just see if went on or in drone compartment and worked ok, It really was slight so would probably work. so tried to put it in my drone. It wouldn't go in the compartment and connect to where I could close the back battery latch on the Yuneec drone. I noticed other reviews said it was snug so I pushed and wiggled trying to get it to go over gold contact points in drone heard a pop like noise at same time it finally went on good. I thought well it was snug but at least it went on. I took the battery back out because was a worried plugs on Yunnec drone contact points might not be able to take that but they looked good from outside. After taking it off and trying it 2nd time it seemed to go on much easier and felt satisfied with the results. It must of bent it straight so would work great and I was good to go now! I thought, (boy was I wrong!) I must of bent that slight crimp straight so it would go on good and seemed to work very good now. I looked in compartment and the contacts where still showing so thought must be ok and it goes on easy now. I went out and tested it flying and I would say it does indeed seem to last longer than the stock battery or just as good, so was impressed by this. I flew it twice and every time was greatly impressed, also used the stock yuneec battery about 3 times during this period and I didn't time it but would say it seemed slightly less, so there battery really can be good. On the 3rd flight my drone was up about 5 minutes from a fully charged battery when the voltage dropped sharply after doing many maneuvers then the low battery warning hit screen at same time drone completely quit flying and plunged to the ground!!! I was super super upset! I paid $1350 for this drone when they where very expensive and didn't need a cheap battery! I thought the battery was bad for sure!!!! This however was not true completely. I 1st had to find the drone that went down over a heavily wooded area. I thought I lost drone after about 3.5 hrs of searching. I went back over areas previously had looked but finally found it. It had crashed through the trees broke all the props and completely ruined my camera I had just previously sent in to Yuneec for repairs and had only got a few flight on it with new repaired camera to find it totally destroyed from the crash again!!!.Yuneec is a great company and repaired the camera for free the 1st time and I think there a great company. It also damaged the landing gear in crashing through trees. I have no idea what internal damage it did to drone. I pulled out the battery to discover that one of the gold leads had been completely disconnected during flight in the Yuneec battery compartment and had dislodged from its mount and was sitting in back of drone!!. I knew for sure because of very poor quality control On MorPilots end that I had inadvertently pushed the plug apparently lose inside Yuneec slightly lose even though battery was still going over it much easier after 1st try. I flew it several times like that with no problem and used Yuneec batteries on several flights with it like this with no problem. I am convinced that the MorPilot battery not fitting on there good caused it to dislodge one of the plugs inside the Yuneec battery hatch for sure because no Yuneec Battery ever was so hard to push in and mount on Yuneed Drone as the Morpilot battery. Also it verified it was indeed the battery that caused it because the same exact contact on Yuneec drone is what was loose and not in battery on the same place the Morpilot battery connection was crimped on. If I had it to do over again there is no way I would have tried to use this battery in the Yuneec drone!!. I will admit I thought the battery was bad and not holding its charge but this is not true. The real problem is that the design flaw caused the Yuneec drone plug side (because was hard to push on) it came lose very slightly and must of wiggled loose over time. I was very upset at the time but trying to be more neutral in observing what happened now. I think Yuneec should make there contact points more secure but at same time I know for sure the Morpilot battery being slightly crimped on one lead caused me to crash the drone and totally destroy camera and rip it off the camera completely and destroy the blades from falling through trees and scraping up body of drone, even though the drone body seems to be intact form such a fall as far as main body and have no idea why it only slightly cracked the landing gear. This bird is a tough bird and trees help soften the crash. I have no idea why the body survived falling approximately 150 feet through trees. I know believe the trees saved it from more damage. I will say that maybe I got a bad batch with poor quality control and most are good batteries, and I will admit it worked well when it did work. And because I see allot of good reviews I do believe it works well allot of times, when it works it probably does work better than stock. But there is no way its worth the price of losing the whole drone because they do not make sure the contacts are not perfectly round and this one had a very small crimp on one of the contacts that I am convinced caused the loss of my drone and looking for it for 3.5 hrs and I am very upset my camera is completely destroyed and I have no idea what kind of internal damage was done to the drone yet so I can't comment on that yet. I hope its ok but do not know at this time. The body looks ok and looks way better than I thought it would from crash despite ruining my camera. However upon removal of the Morpilot battery and seeing 1 lead had somehow wiggled lose in flight and somehow from the crash that plug was completely dislodged now and had ended up further back in drone completely out of its holding bracket I'm convinced if I had never bought this cheap battery my drone would still be flying great in the air today!!. HERE IS THE BIGGEST WARNING OF WHY I AM VERY UPSET THOUGH. My Yuneec IS NOT COVERED UNDER WARANTY IF CRASH IS CAUSED BY A BATTERY OTHER THAN A YUNEEC BATTERY!!!! YOU HAVE TO ALL KNOW THIS!!! and they informed me if I was using a battery other than there battery NOTHING WOULD BE COVERED UNDER WARRANTY FROM USING ANOTHER MANUFACTURES BATTERY CAUSING DAMAGE TO DRONE!!!!. if you want to risk totally losing your drone and voiding your warranty!!!! and just hope you get a good battery from Morpilot good luck. I'm not trying to rip this company up or say they make bad products or don't have good batteries, but the fact Yuneec WILL COVER NOTHING NOW UNDER WARRENTY DO TO FACT I WAS USING ANOTHER MANUFACTURES BATTERY LEAVES ME WITH FULL BILL OF CRASH AND FIXING IT!!!! The only way I know to really check if connections are safe after hard to install is take drone apart. And that voids your warranty and if you send it in to see if it is ok into Yuneec, well Yuneec can't be responsible for damage from something that is not there product. Yuneec has to charge you for all repairs and checks because was not caused by there battery. That is only fair. That is only fair on there part I'm sure. I feel like your playing the lottery and your whole drone is at stake and you could lose it all. If Morpilot had better quality control and you didn't have to worry about this kind of issue it might be worth it. I don't like to live and learn from such expensive mistakes. If I had it to do over again I would send it back when saw the small crimp on one of connection points. It might be bad luck that I had such a horrible experience with there product that might otherwise be ok. BUT ITS NOT WORTH VOIDING MY WARANTY! AND ME GETTING STUCK WITH FULL BILL WHEN MY DRONE GOES DOWN DO TO A very small design flaw on one of there batteries. Its just bad luck it did not completely break lose so it seemed ok and connected and flew find until wiggled out. If it had not had some kind of connection good enough to fly and just not started up there is no way I could have taken off and had this horrible catastrophe happen to me. I was short on money thinking there cheaper and last longer than Yuneec batteries and I'm not rich so worth going with Morpilot but it cost me having my warranty completely voided and me having to figure out when I can get money to get a new camera (AGAIN!) and what I should do to get my lead fixed and not knowing if any other damage was done inside I can't tell for sure. I can't afford all this kind of hassle just to think I'm getting a cheap good deal on a battery. Very upset!!. Already lost lots of great flying time from just getting camera fixed for free from Yuneec 1st time and they did it for free 1st time! but now its crashed and camera is not under warranty with Morpilot battery or the damaged it caused to my drone. Very Very Very upsetting. Drones are very expensive and this kind of incident makes you wonder if a few more minutes of flying time is worth voiding your whole Warranty and having to fix everything and dig up cash to fix this big mess!! I have no idea!!! Very frustrated about the whole Morepilot experience even though to be completely honest I think if get a good battery and are lucky it will probably be happy with your purchase. I don't want to run the company completely into the ground because of my own experience but you have to be honest and let people know the full ramifications of possible problems of using batteries that Yuneec will not warranty, it don't just effect the warranty of the battery it effects the whole drone when it crashes!!! If you do decide to go with this battery, make very sure it goes on easy. I can't say strongly enough if its hard to get the Morpilot battery on or to get on don't trust it will be ok even if it seems ok. After I got it on it must of bent the crimp enough to go on easy because it went on easier after 1st problem but you really needed to take the whole drone apart and make sure the Yuneec connection points have not become dislodged. And this is a very time consuming hard process and will void your warranty also. So then I would suggest you send drone to Yuneec to inspect but when you tell them I'm not sure the leads are in place on Yuneec anymore and I was using a Morpilot battery and they will probably make you pay for shipping both ways and full cost of repairs, so even if you don't crash the drone if it is hard to get the morpilot battery on and your trying to be safe it will still cost you way more than any money you save!!. I guess somehow it got loose on one lead on my drone even though visually it looked ok I'm very confident I jammed it lose somehow even though flew ok many times and it eventually worked lose out of battery causing entire drone to all out of the air.. Very very upsetting!!. I don't know how you will tell if it knocked a battery connector lose or not because I checked and until the drone crashed, had no idea there was a problem and was impressed with what the battery was doing for my flights. From the outside the lead seemed ok but after crash it was apparent it had been jammed lose from pushing too hard to get the Morpilot battery to fit my battery compartment. I can't blame Morpilot completely, but do mostly because it would have never happened if hadn't got this battery. I'm not sure how I would have figured that out? so hard to blame myself either. . This is my honest opinion of Morpilot Batteries. You might like them and if your very unlucky it will cost you way more than its worth to try and save a little money on these batteries. Or is might just be very bad luck on my part, I have no idea if Yuneec might have a few issues with some of there batteries doing the same thing. But at least!!! my WARANTY IS NOT VOIDED!!!! BE AWARE!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Know the full ramifications of what is going on and the reason I'm posting this to Amazon is if it saves one drone from crashing its worth it to me to write this!!.I want others to learn from my mistakes so they don't learn the hard way like I did. ANYONE OUT THERE IF YOUR MORPILOT BATTERY DOES NOT SLIP ON EASY, DO NOT USE IT! AND DO NOT TRUST DRONE IS SAFE TO USE WITH ANY BATTERY NOW UNTIL IT IS FULLY INSPECTED!!! AND BE PREPARED TO SPEND ALLOT OF MONEY AND TIME SHIPPING IT BACK AND FORTH FROM YUNEEC OR FIX IT YOURSELF AND VOID WARANTY!! AND HOPE YOU KNOW WHAT YOUR DOING!!. I AM CONTEMPLATING FIXING IT MYSELF as far as trying to figure out how to take body apart and put lead back in place and see if drone still works, there is no way I can fix the camera for sure, have to buy a whole new unit now and new blades, I CAN'T AFFORD THIS KIND OF CRAP HAPPEING AND NEITHER CAN YOU!! BUYER BE WARE. MAY OR MAY NOT WORK FOR YOU!!! PLEASE PLEASE! MAKE SURE BATTERY GOES ON EASY OR DON'T FORCE IT!! OR DON'T TRY TOO HARD TO MAKE IT WORK. JUST SEND IT BACK!!! If it goes on easy you hopefully are fine. if its kind of hard to put on 1st time and then easy after that. DON'T TRUST YOUR OK!! YOUR NOT!! MINE DROPPED OUT OF AIR AND WORKED ITS WAY LOSE IN AIR SOMEHOW!!!, my opinion is, with only 3 fights to say, it does indeed seem to last longer than the Yuneec batteries or just as long but wasn't worth it at all in my case. This is my very honest opinion. please make informed decisions with honest peoples experiences and don't trust that because reviews show a bunch of good experiences that you cannot receive a faulty product. Be fully informed and you will have a better chance of having a good experience. Based on my honest true short lived experience, all I can say is the battery appears to be good and do what company says if it slips on easy over your connection points in Yuneec drone, but if it is hard to put on your drone, Please send it back! it is NOT worth it!!! And keep in mind, IS THIS BATTERY WORTH LOSING MY DRONE AND VOIDING MY WARANTY? make sure you get a good one or DON'T BUY OR RISK IT AT ALL!!! I've been working with Morpilot customer service and they get mad because I can't understand them and they will not address my issues. They refuse to address the fact I lost my drone and will never reply to pages of emails I sent on this asking them if they have anything to say about it. This is and exact copies of what they say back. to give you an idea how they work on some of there replies.

Morpilot customer service reply

This photo have showed I HAD SENT THE REPLACEMRNT BEFORE YOU REPLIED TO ME.I do not need to lie.If you think I am a Chinese trying to talk in English,and you want to solve the problem,why do not you speak in chinese?If you do want slove it ,just be polit and patient .As the regulation of Amazon ,we are responsible for the cost of battery.Again,This kind of battery is Li-PO battery which have been attested by CE and UL.
Thank you again.

Wow!!! they claim they have sent me a new battery! and will not address tons of questions on how they feel about the fact there battery crashed my drone. I dont' know where this person is from but his image had Chinese writing on it and I couldn't open it and I had hard time following what they say? Is it just me or do you understand this guy? They refuse to address anything they don't want to deal with. I don't like there customer service but in all fairness you might get a good battery and be completely satisfied. But I really really don't like my experience with them. He still hasn't sent me tracking number or answered any of my other questions and I am very unhappy about my experience but in all fairness you might have a great experience. Wow I'm getting possibly a new battery to replace my faulty battery. Wow. even though my drone that cost me 1350.00 dollars crashed and no matter how many time I report this to them they will not address that part.. Buyer be WARE!!!!!! .. may or may not work for you..

I will update this review when I get drone fixed and see what new battery does, it might work great. I hope it is one of the better batteries and I just had bad luck. Oh well, live and learn. I just wish it hadn't cost me such to figure all this out and crash drone. Please Know this potential pitfall when getting any battery for your drone and make sure it slides on easy and is not defective.

That is the lesson I learned the hard way.

here is another response from them exact copy

Morpilot response

Dear friend ,
Sorry to trouble you again.We are also sorry for the worries we brought to you.We have send the reolacement to you.Our product have been tested . It is probably that the battery have a strong impact in transit, causing internal damage. Our goal is to offer high-quality products and good service,which might take a long time to improve.As there are some insignificance,we try our best to rectify it.Just hope you customer would be satisfied.What’s more, as a customer service,we are committed to solve customers’ problems,and your satisfaction is the encouragement to us .We are wondering if you have other question needed to be solved?
Thank you for your understanding!
Here is the Specification:

• Battery Type: Lithium Polymer (LiPO Battery)
• C Rate: 10C
• Volts: 11.1V
• Capacity: 6300mAh
• Cell Count: 3S
• Cell Configuration: 3S1P
• Continuous Discharge: 10C (64A)
• Max Burst Rate: 20C (128A)
• Max Volts per Cell: 4.2V
• Max Volts per Pack: 12.6V
• Min Volts per Pack: 9V
• Charge Rate: 1C (6.4A)
• Dimensions: 179.5 x 50.5 x 33.5 mm / 7.1 x 1.9 x 1.3 in
• Watt Hours: 66.6
• Weight:14.8 oz (420 g)
• Energy Density: 158.6 WH/KG

Hope it makes sense
END OF Morpilot response.

I guess there trying. but is sure is hard for me to understand them completely. Oh well, good luck to all of you out there, hope you have a great experience with there batteries, I did up to a point and hope and will make sure there new replacement battery has good perfectly round connection points after I fix my drone I guess.

I will try to be fair with the actual new replacement battery if it works good after fix drone. To be honest I think there batteries if not damaged should be as good or better than yuneec. but keep in mind. voids your warranty! so be ware! Trying to be fair after all this stress of crashing drone. I will try to be more fair and just judge the new battery and do a different review later maybe. Still upset about what happened at moment. Its hard not to be upset about drone crashing, just need to let you all try to look out for this potential problem so don't happen to you I guess.

update:8-16-2016
Morpilot battery compared to Yunnec battery does not slide over terminals easy, can see now that drone is apart. I believe it is slightly larger than yuneec battery because Yuneec battery always goes on much smoother. Morpilot harder to fit in slot like others have said. Can see with my terminal back in place the Morpilot battery is a tighter fit and this can cause it to force terminal loose potentially if it is hard to plug into the yuneec battery compartment. Much easier to see how this works with drone apart. Everyone check this to make sure its not an issue Please!!! If the Morpilot battery is hard to slide over your terminals it is potentially unsafe in my opinion. So check it to fly safely. Yuneec battery has more wiggle room to fit over the battery terminals inside the drone compartment on my drone for sure and is a much better fit.
top_rant.title: Wish I would have never bought this cheap battery even though worked good for 3 flights with Morpiolot battery before it crashed

top_rant.text:
It’s me! Back again for more punishment! Actually, people seem to be thinking my thorough reviews are helpful, so who am I to stop now?

Before I get into the book itself, I first have to say the titles of these books are like George RR Martin’s book title’s silly cousins.
